Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

<rant> honestly, who would name a new tool "ss" these days? Two-letter command names might have been needed in the 70s when memory was a thousand dollars per KB, but the name is totally meaningless ("show something?") and I fail to see how using "ss" is any better than "netstat"?  Another package to install, and it is now skipping this test on distros that don't have it?</rant>

Patch Set 1:

> <rant> honestly, who would name a new tool "ss" these days? Two-letter command names might have been needed in the 70s when memory was a thousand dollars per KB, but the name is totally meaningless ("show something?") and I fail to see how using "ss" is any better than "netstat"?  Another package to install, and it is now skipping this test on distros that don't have it?</rant>

Yeah... ss stands for 'socket stats'. It exists for a very long time, and have been advertised as the 'netstat' replacement for a while. It comes along with 'ip', which has the same story as an 'ifconfig' replacement. 'iproute2' is available and installed everywhere for so long, that's not really a big deal.

Than, are those tools better? That's a different story, but the history is moving away from ifconfig and netstat for sure...

It looks like this patch could be replaced by the one-liner to fall back to netstat from ss if it isn't installed. Also, a similar fix is needed in setup-kerberos.sh.

commit.subject: LU-13204 tests: replace netstat with ss tool
commit.message:
LU-13204 tests: replace netstat with ss tool

The application netstat has been replaced by ss. Update sanity
test 100 to use ss.

Test-Parameters: clientdistro=sles12sp5 testlist=sanity env=ONLY=100
Change-Id: I3c0e376394786b93de9d57f6f92e9e3480d817f7
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
